customFaq.question: Can I get approved with bad credit?
customFaq.answer: Yes. ReadyCar works with multiple lending partners to help people in all credit situations — bad credit, no credit, bankruptcy, and consumer proposals. Apply online and we will find the best financing option for you.

customFaq.question: Do I need a co-signer?
customFaq.answer: Not necessarily. Many customers get approved on their own. Our financing team will review your application and let you know if a co-signer would improve your rate or approval chances, but it is often not required.

customFaq.question: What is the minimum credit score needed?
customFaq.answer: There is no minimum credit score to apply. We look at your full financial picture, not just a number. Apply online and our team will work to find a solution that fits your situation.

customFaq.question: Will applying hurt my credit score?
customFaq.answer: No. Our initial application uses a soft credit check which has no impact on your credit score. You can apply with no obligation.

customFaq.question: How long does the approval process take?
customFaq.answer: Most applicants receive a same-day decision. The online application takes just a few minutes, and our team typically follows up within hours.

customFaq.question: Can I get approved after bankruptcy or a consumer proposal?
customFaq.answer: Yes. Whether you have been discharged from bankruptcy or are in an active consumer proposal, we have lending partners who work with these situations. A bankruptcy does not have to keep you off the road.

customFaq.question: I am new to Canada. Can I get a car loan?
customFaq.answer: Yes. ReadyCar welcomes newcomers to Canada. Even without Canadian credit history, we can help you get into a vehicle and start building your credit.

customFaq.question: What documents do I need?
customFaq.answer: Start with our quick online application. For final approval, you will generally need proof of income, proof of address, and valid identification. Your advisor will guide you through the specifics.
